meta.reportLabel: Thu Report — INTERMARKET FLIP: escalation holds (WTI ~$74-75, Brent ~$78) but gold/silver SMASHED - XAU ~$4,030-90, XAG ~$57.5-58.5 - as rising oil = higher yields + firm USD beat the haven bid; Fed minutes split (upside risks, cited Hormuz), Doha talks concluded with 'positive progress'; metals flipped to RANGE, USOIL long extending; Jul 14 CPI the arbiter.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The escalation is holding (WTI ~$74-75, Brent ~$78) but the intermarket wiring flipped: rising oil is read as inflation -> higher-for-longer Fed -> rising Treasury yields + firm USD, and that real-rates channel SMASHED gold and silver (XAU ~$4,030-90, lowest since Jul 2; XAG ~$57.5-58.5) despite live strikes. USD/JPY sits pinned at ~162 on yields, not rallying on risk-off. Fed June minutes came split with upside inflation risks (naming Hormuz); the Doha technical talks concluded with 'positive progress' and reconvene after the funeral. Everything is headline-hostage into the Jul 14 CPI.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC: US June CPI - the 4.2%-regime check that decides the Sept hike (~50%); Doha talks reconvene post-funeral is the de-escalation swing

dailyRead: The escalation is holding but the intermarket wiring flipped overnight: WTI pushed to ~$74-75 (Brent ~$78, +5%) as the Hormuz tanker war stayed live, yet gold and silver were SMASHED - XAU fell to ~$4,030-4,090 (lowest since Jul 2) and XAG cratered to ~$57.5-58.5 from ~$61 - because rising oil is being read as inflation -> higher-for-longer Fed -> rising Treasury yields + firm USD, and that real-rates channel overwhelmed the haven bid. Yesterday's gold-long thesis was the casualty; the same rising-yield force is propping USD/JPY at ~162 despite the risk-off tape, so the JPY-strength trades are now backstop (MoF) plays more than momentum plays. The Fed's June minutes landed split with upside inflation risks flagged (explicitly citing Hormuz), yields rose, USD firmed - and the Doha technical talks actually concluded with 'positive progress' and a commitment to reconvene after Khamenei's funeral, so the diplomatic track is alive even as strikes continue. Cleanest expressions: long USOIL (premium extending), short EUR/USD and USD/CAD-long on the firm USD, with the metals flipped to RANGE and the whole board still headline-hostage into the Jul 14 CPI.

today.headline: Rising yields + firm USD as oil re-spike reads as inflation, not just haven
today.read: The June minutes came split but flagged upside inflation risks (naming Hormuz); yields rose and the oil bid re-arms the energy-CPI floor, keeping the Sept hike (~50%) alive. Minutes now behind us - the Jul 14 CPI is the arbiter; DXY holds >101 with a firmer long tilt.

today.headline: Gold SMASHED into the escalation — real-rates channel beats the haven bid
today.read: XAU fell to ~$4,030-4,090 (lowest since Jul 2) because rising oil = higher yields + firm USD overwhelmed haven flow; the LONG is flipped to RANGE. Only a ceasefire that drops yields while keeping risk-off bid revives it - a hot CPI extends the selloff.

today.headline: Yield-pinned at ~162 — strength case narrows to the MoF backstop
today.read: Rising US yields kept spot at ~162 despite risk-off, so the JPY-strength trades are now backstop-asymmetry plays (Y11.73tn precedent), not momentum. USD/JPY and JPYBASKET downgraded; a yield spike to 163+ forces the MoF's hand.

today.headline: Oil floor firms (WTI ~$74-75) but Friday's jobs seen fading to ~+10k
today.read: The oil bid firms the floor under the weakest currency, but June jobs are expected to calm sharply after May's +88k (unemp 6.6%). BoC still sidelined Jul 15; USD/CAD stays a long on the firm-USD leg, with the oil bid the offsetting risk.

today.headline: Risk-off beta + a soft China demand pulse (CPI +1%)
today.read: China CPI missed at +1% and the Aug 11 RBA tail was already thin (~36%), so AUD wears the beta plus a lighter demand story; AUD/JPY, AUD/USD stay short but the yield-pinned JPY caps the downside. Q2 CPI late July is the re-arm date.

today.headline: No change — soft with the core wrinkle; the firm-USD tape does the work
today.read: Flash 2.8% cooling still guts Jul 23 urgency, but core 2.5% plus the oil spike keeps the Sept hike alive - EUR/USD short is the clean USD-leg expression. The squeeze risk is a hawkish Jul 23 tone.

today.headline: No change — re-armed BoE thesis holds; oil adds to the hawks' case
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and the energy spike strengthens their hand. Firm but rangebound vs a firm high-yield USD; long tilt vs AUD.

strength.tag: oil = inflation read lifts yields; minutes split, upside risks
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2% on participation 61.5%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced, Sept ~50%. June minutes came split but flagged upside inflation risk (naming Hormuz); Treasury yields rose and the USD firmed.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; the oil re-spike re-arms the energy-CPI floor and the rising-yield read keeps the Sept tail alive. Minutes now behind us; the Jun CPI (Jul 14) is the arbiter. Risk: a second soft CPI print turns the NFP crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; oil adds to the hawks' case
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); quiet tape, thesis unchanged.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round, and the oil spike adds to the energy-CPI floor. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: 2.50%, first hike since 2023; higher hurdle next
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0 - but the statement was the dovish version: headline inflation now seen PEAKED at 3.9% in Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), 4 of 6 members call risks balanced vs 2 upside. NZD popped and faded; soft China CPI (+1%) trims the growth story.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hike delivered but guidance capped it - Sept still likely, not aggressively priced; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a 4%+ CPI print re-arms the hawks; risk-off beta cuts the other way. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: yield-pinned at ~162; strength case = MoF backstop
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes - oil feeds its inflation case. But rising US yields pin spot at ~162 despite risk-off - deep in the proven MoF zone (Y11.73tn/$74bn precedent) with no intervention yet.
strength.forward: 🦅 The haven bid is real but the rising-yield USD is capping it - the JPY-strength case is now the backstop asymmetry more than momentum; BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. Risk: a rapid de-escalation restores the carry grind; a yield spike to 163+ forces the MoF's hand. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%), China CPI +1% demand pulse light
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); the Aug 11 hike was priced only ~36% before the escalation. China CPI missed at +1%, trimming the demand pulse.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The sticky-core floor stands but risk-off + a soft China pulse hit the beta first - an oil-shock growth scare argues RBA patience through Q3. Risk: fast de-escalation plus a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) flips it straight back to hawkish. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core 2.5%; firm-USD tape
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting; the firm-USD tape does the work on EUR/USD.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core plus the oil re-spike keep a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on core + energy squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: WTI ~$74-75 firms the floor; jobs seen fading Friday
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May +88k (June jobs Friday, consensus ~+10k); wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI ~$74-75 firms the floor under the board's weakest currency.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Still the softest macro on the board but the oil bid firms the floor; BoC sidelined Jul 15 (core ~2% vs energy headline); Friday's June jobs (~+10k exp) is the domestic catalyst. Risk: de-escalation re-opens the glut and re-pins CAD; a soft jobs print does the same. Next BoC Jul 15.

symbols.risk: The core-2.5% wrinkle turns Jul 23 hawkish; de-escalation fades the USD bid.

symbols.risk: Oil holds >$75 and CAD squeezes; a jobs beat; soft US CPI Jul 14.

symbols.risk: A credible Doha breakthrough crushes the premium fast - headline-hostage, size small.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a delivered-but-dovish RBNZ - no vector post-event.

symbols.why: Long tilt firmed: rising yields + a hawkish-split minutes read hold DXY above 101 and EUR (57.6% weight) is soft, but Sept is still only ~50% so it grinds - Jul 14 CPI is the trigger.
symbols.risk: De-escalation unwinds the haven bid; a soft CPI Jul 14.

symbols.why: Downgraded from LONG MED: the haven bid is intact but rising US yields pin JPY at ~162 with no intervention - now a backstop-asymmetry play, not a momentum long. (Rises when JPY strengthens - opposite ticket to XXX/JPY.)
symbols.risk: A yield spike drags it lower and forces the MoF; de-escalation caps it.

symbols.why: Oil-glut tailwind still reversed (WTI ~$74-75), geopolitics a direct headwind, and the index is stretched at record territory (~25,800).
symbols.risk: De-escalation restores the melt-up; a deeper oil spike turns it SHORT.

symbols.why: FLIPPED from LONG: the escalation SOLD gold - XAU fell to ~$4,030-4,090 (lowest since Jul 2) as rising oil fed the higher-yields read and the firm USD + real-rates channel beat the haven bid.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire that drops yields AND keeps risk-off bid revives the long; a hot CPI extends the selloff.

symbols.why: Hit harder than gold: XAG cratered to ~$57.5-58.5 from ~$61 - the industrial leg (ratio ~68) plus rising real yields overwhelmed any haven flow in an oil-shock growth scare.
symbols.risk: A high-beta flush lower; follows gold only if the haven leg re-dominates.

catalysts.note: CPI soft (demand pulse light for AUD/NZD); PPI 4-yr high on oil pass-through; funeral vacuum closes with strikes live.
Consensus ~+10k, unemp 6.6% - whether May's +88k was noise; feeds a CAD that just got an oil bid, ahead of BoC Jul 15.
Confirmation print; EZ inflation texture into ECB Jul 23.
Technical round concluded with 'positive progress'; the next sit is the first hard de-escalation signal - oil, CAD, JPY, gold all key off it.
The 4.2%-regime check - re-arms or kills the Sept hike (~50%); the oil re-spike raises the bar for a soft print.
Hold; the oil re-spike vs core ~2% and a technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% + fresh energy-shock language is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; Sept ~50% hangs on the Jul 14 CPI.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%; oil adds to the hawks' case.
Guidance with spot ~162, the MoF zone hot, and oil re-feeding its inflation case.
Iran: fees 'definitely'; Washington: rejected - now with active strikes in between.
3 vessels hit, CENTCOM strikes ongoing, sanctions reimposed; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold.

geopolitics: The escalation is holding but the intermarket transmission inverted. After Tuesday's strikes on the Al Rekayat and Wedyan and the IRGC's hit on a third vessel, CENTCOM's retaliation continued and Treasury's reimposed oil sanctions stayed in force; WTI pushed to ~$74-75 and Brent to ~$78 (+5%). Crucially, the Doha technical track did not collapse - Qatar and Pakistan reported 'positive progress' on the Islamabad MoU and committed to reconvene after Khamenei's funeral processions, even as Trump publicly called the June-17 deal 'over' and roughly 6,000 seafarers remain stranded in the Gulf. The FX read that dominated overnight was not the classic haven bid but the RATES channel: rising oil -> higher inflation expectations -> a higher-for-longer Fed (the June minutes, released split, explicitly flagged upside inflation risk from Hormuz) -> rising Treasury yields and a firmer USD. That is why gold and silver were sold hard despite live strikes - XAU to ~$4,030-4,090, XAG to ~$57.5-58.5 - and why USD/JPY sits pinned at ~162 rather than rallying on risk-off. Transmission by currency: USD is the cleanest beneficiary (yields + haven); CAD gets a floor from oil but faces a soft jobs print Friday; JPY's strength case narrows to the MoF backstop while yields cap it; AUD and NZD wear the risk-off beta PLUS a soft China demand pulse (CPI +1%); EUR is soft on the firm-USD tape; GER40 loses its oil tailwind at record levels. The de-escalation scenario is very much alive - the talks progressing means a Doha breakthrough could crush the premium (and, by dropping yields, revive gold) as fast as it built - which is why the board still carries no high-conviction pairs and the oil-linked calls are sized small.
